title: Reference Card
subtitle: Screenshot this — a cheat sheet for the three settings.

rows.side_effect: Background goes blurry (shallow depth of field)

technical.name: Aperture
technical.detail: f/1.8 = wide open, f/22 = very closed. Lower number = wider opening (it's a fraction).

rows.side_effect: Moving subjects streak across the frame (technical term: *motion blur*)

technical.name: Shutter speed
technical.detail: 1/500s = short, 1/30s = long. Fractions of a second for most daytime shots.

technical.name: ISO
technical.detail: ISO 100 = low, ISO 6400 = high. Doubles with each stop (100 → 200 → 400…).

vocabulary_bridge: Throughout the lessons we used *motion streaking* to describe the long-shutter blur of moving subjects. The technical term photographers use is *motion blur* — same idea, different label. Keeping the word "blur" scoped to this reference card avoids confusion with the separate blurry-background effect from a wide iris (shallow depth of field).
closing: The best photographers don't memorize rules — they develop intuition for which tradeoff serves each moment.
